title: Freshly Spherified Slovakian Amazake Dumplings
description: A refreshing and herbal post-workout Slovakian dish featuring amazake, this dairy-free friendly recipe is perfect for a snack, utilizing the spherification technique to create a unique and snappy texture. The combination of amazake and herbal ingredients provides a boost of energy and flavor.
instructions: In a large bowl, mix together the chilled amazake, sodium alginate, and chopped fresh parsley and dill until well combined.
In a separate large bowl, mix together the water and calcium chloride until the calcium chloride is fully dissolved.
Using a syringe or piping bag, drop small amounts of the amazake mixture into the calcium chloride bath, creating small spheres.
Let the spheres sit in the calcium chloride bath for about 10 minutes to fully set.
Remove the spheres from the calcium chloride bath with a slotted spoon and rinse them gently with cold water.
In a small bowl, mix together the lemon juice and maple syrup until well combined.
Serve the freshly spherified amazake dumplings with the lemon juice and maple syrup dressing.
Garnish with additional chopped fresh parsley and dill if desired.
Store any leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.
